states.description: Android home screen with stopwatch/timer widget visible
visual_cues: timer/stopwatch widget showing time like '00:-12' or '00:22'

states.description: Clock app main Clock tab showing time and date
Stopwatch tab with timer at 00 and Start button
Timer setup screen with numeric keypad

transitions.postcondition: opens clock app to stopwatch tab
clock app opened to default tab

strategies.name: PAUSE_RUNNING_STOPWATCH_FROM_HOME
strategies.preconditions: home screen shows running stopwatch widget
steps: click the stopwatch widget/timer display on home screen
verify stopwatch tab opens showing Start button (indicates paused state)
strategies.success_signal: stopwatch screen shows 'Start' button and '00' display (paused state)
strategies.fallback: if landed elsewhere, navigate to Stopwatch tab and inspect state

strategies.name: START_STOPWATCH
steps: click the stopwatch widget on home screen to open clock app on stopwatch tab (or open Clock app and click Stopwatch tab)
verify time is counting up and Pause/Reset/Lap buttons appear
strategies.success_signal: Pause button visible and elapsed time increases
strategies.fallback: if on wrong tab, tap Stopwatch in bottom nav

strategies.name: ENTER_TIMER_DIGITS
strategies.preconditions: clock_main visible; target duration known as H:MM:SS
steps: from clock_main, click the Timer tab to enter timer_entry
expand the target duration into a left-padded 6-digit string HHMMSS (e.g. 1h 23m 40s -> '012340')
tap each digit in order left-to-right; the keypad uses odometer shift-fill semantics — the first tap lands in the least-significant position and shifts left as more digits arrive
after all 6 digits, verify the display reads the target HH:MM:SS exactly (not a shifted variant like 00:12:34 when the goal was 01:23:40)
if the display does not match, tap ⌫ to remove the trailing digit and re-align, or clear fully and re-enter from scratch
click Start to begin the countdown
strategies.success_signal: the timer begins counting down from a value within 1 second of the target; the Start button is replaced by Pause/Cancel affordances
strategies.fallback: If the digit display never aligns to the target after two full re-entries, emit status:infeasible — do NOT invent indices or try to type directly into HH/MM/SS fields (they are not editable text, only via the shift-fill keypad).

dead_ends.note: Agent assumed task was already done by reading widget state; must actually perform the action or verify end-state deterministically

dead_ends.failed_action: clicking digits assuming they fill hours first
dead_ends.note: Timer keypad fills seconds first and digits shift left as new digits are entered (like an odometer). Agent repeatedly misinterpreted order, causing wrong values.
Backspace only removes last digit (and shifts right), not a full clear; repeated wrong digit entries compounded the error

categories.name: TOGGLE_STATE
categories.precondition: An entity exists with a toggleable state (e.g., active/inactive, on/off) and is visible or reachable
abstract_steps: Identify current state via visual indicators
Navigate to the control surface if not already there
Verify the state changed to the desired target
failure_modes: Declaring success without taking any action based on a misread of current state
Clicking a non-interactive label instead of the actual control
Toggling past the desired state (e.g., invoking the toggle on an already-target-state item flips it back to the opposite state)
verification_checklist: Target state's distinctive UI element is present (e.g., opposite action button shown)

categories.name: ACTIVATE_ENTITY
categories.precondition: Entity is in an inactive/idle state and can be transitioned to an active state
abstract_steps: Navigate to the entity's control screen
Verify active-state controls appear (e.g., controls for suspending or terminating the active state)
failure_modes: Attempting activation on the wrong tab/section
Mistaking a display for a button

categories.name: ENTER_STRUCTURED_VALUE
categories.precondition: An input surface exists that accepts a multi-field structured value (e.g., H:M:S, date parts) via a keypad that shifts digits
abstract_steps: Understand the keypad semantics: determine whether digits fill from the right (shift-left) or whether fields are edited independently
If shift-fill: enter the full digit sequence in order from most-significant to least-significant, including leading zeros to place digits in correct fields
Avoid partial backspaces unless correcting the last digit only
Verify the displayed value matches the target exactly before confirming
failure_modes: Assuming each field is edited independently when keypad actually shifts digits across fields
Entering only significant digits and skipping leading zeros, causing digits to land in the wrong field
Using backspace repeatedly to 'clear' without realizing it only removes one digit at a time
Re-entering after partial clear and compounding misalignment
Declaring completion before verifying display matches target
verification_checklist: Displayed value exactly equals target across all sub-fields
Did not inadvertently trigger an activation/confirm control

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO_SECTION
categories.precondition: App has multiple tabs/sections and a bottom/top nav is visible
abstract_steps: Open the app if not already open
Identify the target tab by label or icon
Verify title/contents match the desired section
failure_modes: Clicking a home-screen widget that opens a different tab than intended
Mistaking section labels across similar tabs
